title: Plotting inequalities
description: Create  number line graphs of inequalities.

hints:
The $>$ symbol means "greater than and not including".

A filled circle means "including this number".

An open circle means "not including this number".

$x > -3$ does *not* include $-3$. We can draw an open circle at $-3$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Numbers to the *right* of $-3$ are greater than $-3$. We can draw an arrow to the right of the circle.

The graph of $x>-3$ is:

[[☃ number-line 1]]

options.explanation:
Drag the circle to values on the number line.

Use the buttons to switch arrow directions and to make the circle open or filled.
options.showPrompt: How do I graph the inequality?

hints:
The $<$ symbol means "less than and not including".

A filled circle means "including this number".

$x < 1$ does *not* include $1$. We can draw an open circle at $1$.


[[☃ image 1]]

Numbers to the *left* of $1$ are less than $1$. We can draw an arrow to the left of the circle.

The graph of $x<1$ is:

hints:
The $\geq$ symbol means "greater than or equal to".

A filled circle means "including this number".

An open circle means "not including this number".

$x \geq -2$ includes $-2$. We can draw a filled circle at $-2$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Numbers to the *right* of $-2$ are greater than $-2$. We can draw an arrow to the right of the circle.

The graph of $x\geq-2$ is:

[[☃ number-line 2]]
